mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X7 xDrive40i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B58 engine typically costs £4,000–£7,500 supply only depending on which generation (pre-LCI or LCI), with used units available from £2,000–£4,500. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on the workshop. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X7 xDrive40i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. It's a significant improvement over the N55 and shares its architecture with the bulletproof B58 used across BMW's range and in the Toyota Supra. The main weaknesses are the oil filter housing (plastic, can crack), cooling system components, and carbon build-up on intake valves — all are well-understood and repairable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X7 xDrive40i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oil filter housing cracking (causing coolant and/or oil leaks — BMW faces a class action lawsuit over this), cooling system leaks (water pump and hoses), and carbon build-up on intake valves. The B58 is fundamentally robust — these are maintenance items, not catastrophic design flaws.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X7 xDrive40i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: X7 values remain strong — a well-kept example is often worth £30,000–£60,000+. A £4,000–£7,500 reconditioned engine is only 10–20% of the vehicle's value — well within the 70% BMW threshold. Replacement is almost always worthwhile on a cherished example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X7 xDrive40i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. G07 X7 (2019–2022) uses B58B30C; G07 LCI X7 (2022+) uses B58B30P. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the B58 oil filter housing, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: It needs replacement — the plastic housing cracks and cannot be reliably repaired. Replacement costs £800–£1,800 and is labour-intensive due to the intake manifold needing removal. Upgraded metal housings are available and recommended. BMW faces a class action lawsuit over this issue. If you notice coolant or oil loss, have it inspected immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X7 xDrive40i Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current X7 xDrive40i market. Pre-LCI B58 engines sit at the lower end; LCI B58P examples command a premium. See the Era Map above for generation-specific context.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X7 xDrive40i engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The X7's size and the B58's complexity make it a specialist job.

problems.title: Oil Filter Housing Cracking (B58)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, oil spots under the car, low oil pressure warning, engine warning light, sweet smell from the engine bay, mixing of oil and coolant
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (oil filter housing replacement, including intake manifold removal)
problems.recommendation: The B58's oil filter housing is a known weak point. BMW chose to construct it out of plastic, and the housing tends to split if the cap is tightened too tight or even just over time as the plastic becomes brittle from numerous heat cycles. BMW is facing a class action lawsuit for faulty oil filter housings that leak coolant, resulting in owners having to spend thousands of dollars in repairs and replacements. Because it sits under the intake manifold, replacement is labour-intensive. Upgraded metal housings are available. Check for oil spots or coolant loss regularly, and investigate any drop in fluid levels promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil filter housing failure guide

problems.title: Cooling System Leaks (Water Pump and Hoses)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, rising temperature gauge, warning light, steam from the engine bay, heater not working properly
problems.recommendation: Like the B58's predecessor, the cooling system is a common weak point. The electric water pump and plastic coolant hoses can fail over time. The X7's size and weight put additional strain on the cooling system. Replacing the water pump and hoses preventatively at around 70,000–80,000 miles is cheaper than dealing with an overheated engine. Check coolant levels regularly.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: Carbon Build-up on Intake Valves (Direct Injection)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, reduced fuel economy, engine warning light, misfire codes
problems.recommendation: Like all direct-injection engines, the B58 suffers from carbon build-up on the intake valves. This is because fuel is injected directly into the cylinder, so the intake valves don't get washed by fuel. Russian specialists note that fluctuating idle after 100,000 km (62,000 miles) is often caused by carbon build-up on the intake valves. Walnut blasting is the standard cleaning method and is well-understood by BMW specialists. Regular use of quality fuel and occasional Italian tune-ups can help reduce build-up.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: X7 xDrive40i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Labour-intensive but well-understood; upgraded metal housing available
The B58 is far more reliable than earlier chains — but tensioners can still wear
Rare on the B58 — but if it happens, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X7 xDrive40i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with correct BMW-spec oil — the B58 is reliable but unforgiving of neglect
* Evidence of cooling system maintenance — water pump or hoses replaced
* LCI model (2022+) — the B58P with MHEV brings meaningful improvements
* A well-maintained example where the previous owner understood the B58's needs
avoidIf: * Evidence of repeated coolant top-ups without a diagnosed leak — the oil filter housing or cooling system is failing
* Poor service history — the B58 is sensitive to oil quality
* Any engine warning light without a clear diagnosis
* Evidence of oil and coolant mixing — the oil filter housing has failed

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The B58 is one of BMW's finest modern engines — a genuine step forward in reliability from the N55 that preceded it. The X7 xDrive40i combines this exceptional straight-six with the luxury and space of BMW's flagship SUV. Its weaknesses are well-understood: the oil filter housing (plastic, can crack), cooling system components, and carbon build-up. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and the B58 will reward you with exceptional performance and longevity. The LCI's B58P with 48V mild-hybrid is the most refined version of this engine.

cta.label: → Compare xDrive40i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X7 xDrive40i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (oil filter housing or cooling system warning)
2. Oil Condition — Any signs of coolant contamination? (milky appearance — oil filter housing failure)
3. Service History — including which generation (pre-LCI or LCI) and evidence of regular maintenance
4. Cold Start — Any rattles? (timing chain issues are rare on B58 but possible)
5. Idle Quality — Any rough idle or hesitation? (carbon build-up or injector issues)
6. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
7. Turbo Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation, whistling, or wastegate rattle?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst, O2 sensors)
